body.purpose: All prose; ~70ch lines inside the 42rem column.

components.name: Play Button
components.description: The one terracotta action per game card; an anchor styled as a stamped-clay button.
components.css: .ds-btn-play { display: inline-block; background: #8b3a2b; color: #faf6f0; text-decoration: none; padding: 0.6rem 1.4rem; border-radius: 6px; font-weight: bold; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif; transition: background 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-btn-play:hover { background: #6c2d20; } .ds-btn-play:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #8b3a2b; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Game Card
components.description: One bordered card per game: name, short rules prose, Play button. Flat, thin Muted Clay border, no shadow.
components.html:
align3 — Terni Lapilli & Tamil kattam

Three men's morris for two players: place three stones, then slide them along the lines until someone lines up all three.

Play align3 free
components.css: .ds-card-game { border: 1px solid #6b5f58; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1.25rem 1.5rem; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif; color: #2b2320; background: #faf6f0; line-height: 1.6; } .ds-card-game h3 { margin-top: 0; font-size: 1.1rem; } .ds-btn-play { display: inline-block; background: #8b3a2b; color: #faf6f0; text-decoration: none; padding: 0.6rem 1.4rem; border-radius: 6px; font-weight: bold; margin-top: 0.5rem; }

components.name: Section Heading
components.description: Terracotta serif h2; the only place besides links and buttons where the accent appears.
components.css: .ds-heading { font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif; font-size: 1.4rem; font-weight: 700; color: #8b3a2b; margin: 0; }

components.name: FAQ Entry
components.description: Semantic definition-list Q&A; bold question, plain answer, no accordion.
components.html: <dl class="ds-faq"><dt>Do I need an account or app to play?</dt><dd>No. Every game runs directly in the browser with no download, no sign-up, and no ads.</dd></dl>
components.css: .ds-faq { font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif; color: #2b2320; line-height: 1.6; margin: 0; } .ds-faq dt { font-weight: bold; margin-top: 1.25rem; } .ds-faq dd { margin: 0.25rem 0 0; }

narrative.overview: A board drawn in warm sand, played with pebbles and tamarind seeds. Everything on this page should feel handmade, warm, and tactile: paper the color of dry sand, ink the color of dark earth, one terracotta accent like fired clay. The page is a single quiet column of serif prose, the way a good museum postcard explains an artifact in a few honest sentences. The system explicitly rejects the ad-heavy game portal, the generic SaaS landing template, the dark gamer aesthetic, and the kids' cartoon site. Its lightness is literal: one static HTML file, system serif, no images required to render.
keyCharacteristics: Warm tinted neutrals; nothing is pure black or pure white
One terracotta accent doing all the color work (Restrained strategy)
Serif throughout; hierarchy by size and weight, not font changes
Flat surfaces, thin borders, no shadows, no motion

rules.name: The Fired Clay Rule
rules.body: Terracotta appears on at most 10% of the page: links, h2 headings, and Play buttons. If terracotta starts covering backgrounds or large panels, it has escaped the kiln.

rules.name: The No Pure Rule
rules.body: #000 and #fff are forbidden. Every neutral is tinted toward the warm brown hue.

rules.name: The One Family Rule
rules.body: Georgia carries everything. Hierarchy comes from size and weight only; introducing a second family needs a reason as strong as a new game launch.

rules.name: The Sand Is Flat Rule
rules.body: A board drawn in sand casts no shadows. Neither does this page.

dos: Do keep the whole page one static HTML file with inline CSS; no build step, no external fonts or scripts.
Do give every game exactly one terracotta Play link, with an honest label ("Play align3 free").
Do support both schemes via prefers-color-scheme; every color has a light and dark value.
Do hold AA contrast in both schemes, including Paper-on-Terracotta button text.
Do let history do the persuading: specific, true detail over superlatives.
donts: Don't build an "ad-heavy game portal": no thumbnail grids, no screaming CTAs, no clutter.
Don't use the "generic SaaS landing" template: no hero gradients, no feature-card triptychs, no testimonials.
Don't go "dark gamer": no neon on black, no glow effects.
Don't go "kids' cartoon": no bubble letters, no mascots, no primary-color overload.
Don't use #000 or #fff, box-shadows, gradient text, or colored side-stripe borders.
Don't add motion; if any is ever added, it must respect prefers-reduced-motion.
